title: Anti-Sith bill
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Law |relatedevents=[[Jedi Civil War]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' |proposedby= |image=[[File:RevanMalak-TEA.jpg]] |name=Anti-Sith bill
{{C|Darth Revan and Darth Malak, whose war against the Republic motivated the drafting of the bill, depicted}} |dateproposed=c. [[3956 BBY]] |government=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |legislativebody=[[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] |result=The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] religion and affiliated organizations are outlawed |side1= |side2= |otherkeyindividuals=*[[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]
{{C|[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]}} *[[Darth Malak]]
{{C|Dark Lord of the Sith}} }} {{Quote|But the fact remains, the Sith and their teachings were banned by the Senate. Their very existence is a violation of Republic law—and with good reason.|A Republic [[Commander/Legends|commander]] during the New Sith Wars|}} In the latter stages of the [[Old Sith Wars]], the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] approved '''a bill that banned the Sith religion'''. The bill was proposed in response to the carnage the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] inflicted upon [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] of [[3959 BBY|3959]]–[[3956 BBY]]. The legislation was approved, and banned not only the religion, but all affiliated organizations and governments, as well as the teachings of the Sith philosophy. It remained in effect from the end of the Jedi Civil War until the [[New Sith Wars]], during which it was used as justification for the Republic's battle against the [[New Sith]] Empire and its successor organization, the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. It was apparently nullified sometime during the [[Great Peace of the Republic]], as by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]] held provisions that specifically outlawed persecution based on religious affiliation. ==Description== {{Quote|The Sith are an illegal organization.|A Republic [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]]|}} The bill was crafted to outlaw the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]], a religious organization of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] devoted to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and committed to dominating [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The approval of the legislation resulted in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] banning of the Sith religion, its teachings, and all affiliated organizations from the galaxy. ==History== {{Quote|The Senate passed a bill outlawing them nearly three thousand years ago, shortly after Revan and Malak brought destruction to the entire galaxy.|A Republic lieutenant|}} [[File:MalakSithEmpire.png|thumb|left|250px|Darth Malak's Sith forces nearly destroyed the Republic in the Jedi Civil War.]] After their discovery by the Republic in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the Sith dedicated themselves to the goal of destroying the Galactic Republic and its guardians, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Their mission to do so began with the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], and despite the failure of that endeavor, Sith devotees continued to reinvigorate the religion and build organizations based around the philosophy of the dark side. [[Time/Legends|Time]] and time again, the Sith rose up to challenge the Republic throughout the period that became known as the [[Old Sith Wars]]. Despite the repeated victories of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and Republic, the Sith and the societies they established were able to inflict heavy damage upon both groups in every conflict.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' In [[3959 BBY]], the Sith returned yet again to challenge the Republic in a conflict known to history as the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Led by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]], a newly formed [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] devastated the Jedi Order and crippled the Republic. The seemingly inevitable victory of the Sith was [[Battle of Rakata Prime|stopped]] only by Revan himself, who [[Light side of the Force/Legends|turned away]] from the dark side and defeated his former comrade, Malak. Despite surviving yet another vicious battle with the Sith, the Republic and Jedi were almost irreparably damaged, and remained in a state of crisis for a [[Dark Wars|further five years]]. In response to the brutal predations of Revan, Malak, and their followers, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] of the Republic drafted a bill that was intended to outlaw the practice of Sith philosophy. The bill was approved and put into effect, making it illegal from that point onward to establish or be a member of a Sith organization or to partake in the advancement of Sith teachings. The law remained in effect for the next three thousand years, with the Senate maintaining its legal stance against the Sith throughout the [[Great Galactic War]], the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], and the [[New Sith Wars]], all of which were fought against various Sith organizations.''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' indicates that the bill had been in effect for approximately 3,000 years in [[1003 BBY]], meaning that its legal standing was maintained throughout the period that included these conflicts. Near the close of the New Sith Wars, in a conflict called the [[Light and Darkness War]], the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] needed new recruits to continue the fight against the Sith, who were at the time organized into the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. Republic recruiters used the anti-Sith legislation of three thousand years prior to justify their fight against the Brotherhood, as well as to explain the stance of the Jedi in the conflict. [[File:Ruusan.jpg|thumb|right|170px|The New Sith Wars, fought under the legal mandates of the anti-Sith bill]] The New Sith Wars and the Light and Darkness War ended in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] with the supposed extinction of the Sith at the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]]. During the newfound [[Great Peace of the Republic]], most of the galaxy was convinced that the Sith would never return. At some point prior to the [[19 BBY/Legends|end]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the anti-Sith bill was somehow nullified, and the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]] which shaped the Republic was made to include a provision that specifically prohibited persecution based on religious or philosophical backgrounds—which included the practices of the Sith.[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]] ==Controversy== {{Quote|You're a Sith Lord!''"
"''Am I? Even if true, that's hardly a crime. My philosophical outlook is a personal matter. In fact—the last time I read the Constitution, anyway—we have very strict laws against this type of persecution. So I ask you again: what is my alleged crime?|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, in a recording presented to the Senate on [[Empire Day/Legends|Empire Day]], 19 BBY|}} Although the law was in effect for at least three thousand years, its mandate against creating Sith organizations was repeatedly disregarded by those devoted to the Sith philosophy, which led to several galaxy-spanning conflicts between the Republic and the illegally established fraternities. Despite being cited by Republic personnel as a motivation behind their struggles against the Sith, not everyone in the galaxy was convinced that the legislation justified the violence. On the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Apatros/Legends|Apatros]], Republic recruiters used both the illegality of the Brotherhood of Darkness and the supposed moral high ground of the Republic while attempting to validate their crusades against the Sith in the New Sith Wars. As explained by the [[Outer Rim Oreworks Company]] [[Miner/Legends|miner]] [[Darth Bane/Legends|Dessel]], much of the Outer Rim agreed with the Sith belief that the strong should rule the weak, as life on the Rim was far harsher than it was in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] of the Republic. Dessel also stipulated that if the Sith were in charge of the galaxy, they would have a legal stance against the Jedi similar to that of the Republic's against the Sith. During the Great Peace of the Republic, the law had been in some way nullified, with Republic law ultimately indicating that any kind of religious persecution was legally inexcusable.{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_2.html|Area Woman Finds Sith Icon in Pastebread}} In [[22 BBY]],[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #49]] provides a [[Great ReSynchronization]] date of 13:3:28. This date corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] on the [[Galactic Standard Calendar]], according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]] [[Detective/Legends|detective]] [[Dherik Rule]] noted that there was no crime to be pursued when a [[Latika Garboren|woman]] claimed to have discovered a Sith emblem in a dollop of [[pastebread]]. Despite this, the Jedi Order maintained their own stance against the Sith, which [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]—the alter ego of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Darth Sidious—used to vilify the Jedi during his rise to the position of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]. ==Behind the scenes== The only mention of the anti-Sith bill to date is in the [[2006]] novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', written by [[Drew Karpyshyn]]. In the [[2005]] [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], Chancellor Palpatine states that the Republic has strict laws against the kind of persecution outlined by the anti-Sith bill. This indicates that the bill was removed from Republic law prior to 19 BBY, the year in which the book takes place. ==Appearances== *''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Path of Destruction (audiobook)}} {{1stm|in book}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Proyecto de Ley Anti-Sith |pl=Ustawa antysithiańska |esFA=1 }} [[Category:Galactic Republic laws and acts]] [[Category:Jedi Civil War laws and acts]] [[Category:Sith culture]]
